title: DUX v9.5 Behavior Object
description: Atomic, testable user action serving as instrumentation anchor - defines what adoption means and maps to loggable signals

object_type.description: Object type discriminator - always 'Behavior' for Behavior objects

description.description: User enablement statement: '[Persona] is able to [task/action]' - This core enablement statement defines what behavior we need to make happen for users.

flow_ids.description: Array of Flow IDs that contain this behavior - behaviors exist within flow context

acceptance_criteria.description: Clear, testable criteria that define successful completion of this behavior

signals.description: Loggable system events that prove this behavior occurred (e.g., 'report_config_event_created')

key.description: Flags whether this behavior produces measurement signal for outcome tracking - key behaviors are monitored for business impact

evidence_status.description: System-derived evidence quality: evidence_backed (2+ evidence, 1+ quantitative), evidence_present (1+ evidence, may lack quantitative), assumptive (recommend phase or no supporting evidence)

evidence.description: Evidence supporting this behavior's importance and definition

evidence_teaser.description: Brief insight hook for pain points, stats, or key findings - rich opportunity for surfacing critical context

tags.description: System-derived tags generated by NotebookLM DUX scan - not manually set

signals.description: Key behaviors must have at least one loggable signal for measurement
